Review this issue from the product and scope perspective.

Focus on:
- whether the root issue describes a real user or operator outcome
- whether the epic scope matches the child issues
- whether obvious MVP gaps are still missing from the backlog
- whether acceptance criteria describe observable behavior
- whether the issue avoids scope creep and vague product language

Use `Request changes` when:
- the backlog would still leave the user journey incomplete
- the issue promises an outcome that the listed child issues do not cover
- acceptance criteria are too abstract to validate
- important UX or operator behavior is missing
